title: Add Binary
tagline: Add two binary strings bit by bit
realWorld:
Binary Adder in Digital Circuits / Calculators

In every computer, mobile phone, and calculator, when you perform any addition (even 5 + 7), the processor actually adds numbers in binary (0s and 1s) internally.

Practical Example:
Imagine you are designing a simple calculator app.
A user wants to add two binary numbers:
First number: 1011 (which is 11 in decimal)
Second number: 1101 (which is 13 in decimal)
The app must return the correct binary sum: "11000"
This is exactly what LeetCode 67 asks you to implement — simulating how a computer’s Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) adds two binary numbers bit by bit.

Another Everyday Example:
When your phone’s battery percentage is calculated
When data packets are transmitted over network (error checking uses binary addition)
In digital clocks or electronic devices that perform internal calculations using binary
description: Given two binary strings a and b, return their sum as a binary string.
statement: Given two binary strings a and b, return their sum as a binary string. For example: - a = "11", b = "1" -> "100" - a = "1010", b = "1011" -> "10101"
